text.text: What an absolute beautiful experience.  I'm a native NY'er and we often don't take advantage of all the beautiful sights in our own backyard.  I'm so glad I did.  It's such a beautiful building.  It excuses elegance.  The staff is extremely helpful.  Especially since I had .y sister that is in a wheelchair.  They made a very anxious filled night so calm.  Thank you so much for making her feel so welcomed.  We saw Porgy & Bess and it was a beautiful show.  The cast was phenomenal!! Thanks and I'll see you soon

text.text: It was simply magical. It was my first time going to the opera and I am forever in love with the space and the genre. I can't express how enchanting the experience was. The acoustics were perfect, and the atmosphere was absolutely captivating. I will cherish this memory forever.

originalText.text: It was simply magical. It was my first time going to the opera and I am forever in love with the space and the genre. I can't express how enchanting the experience was. The acoustics were perfect, and the atmosphere was absolutely captivating. I will cherish this memory forever.

text.text:
Everything about the Metropolitan Opera House is absolutely exquisite. From the moment you arrive, the grandeur takes your breath away the stunning venue, rich décor, and iconic grand staircase alone are worth the visit.

If you’re under 40, the Under 40s party is beyond expectations. Incredible energy, amazing people, fantastic food and drinks, and endless photo ops. You also get a discount on tickets so if you’re 40 or younger look into this.

Get dressed up! Truly one of the most fun and stylish ways to experience the opera. This is the place for it. Arrive early to take photos, and if you can, make a night of it with dinner beforehand.

There is honestly not a bad seat in the house. Sightlines are excellent, the seat was comfy and I especially loved the subtitle translations, which make the performance even more immersive and accessible.

Entering and exiting the venue was smooth, easy, and felt very safe well organized and welcoming.

The only downside: they allow people to enter very late nearly 30 minutes after the performance starts and it was incredibly disruptive. Unfortunately, some late arrivals were also quite rude, taking a long time to settle and frequently blocking the view of others.

That said, the overall experience is unforgettable. Elegant, exciting, and truly world-class. A must-do NYC experience. 🎭✨ @thesequinunicorn reviews

text.text:
I was lucky to have been to this wonderful theater some time ago.
My performance was the iconic Swan Lake by Tchaikovsky.
I saw different Swan Lake performances in other theaters, including The Bolshoi; however, I cannot call myself a connoisseur.
I just like the ballet -)

It was hard to get tickets a couple of weeks in advance (which was our case) and we used a website that resells tickets.
Fortunately, our tickets were valid -)

You can treat yourself to a glass of champagne before the performance and during the interval.

As for the hall itself, I was in the stalls and the view was quite good - please check out the photo.

The performance was really great, true and passionate, and what I liked in Met Opera especially was the audience.
It is the first theater with the audience that is so emotional about the finale - there were several bursts of applause during the last 10-15 minutes and, of course, a standing ovation afterwards.
Fantastic night and such a powerful energy load!

I wish I could visit the Metropolitan Opera again.
When (and if at all)? Who knows...

text.text: Attending an OPERA was not ever something I thought I would be doing this year, but I couldn't resist going to see Michael Adams performing in Carmen before he had his last performance! I went on a Saturday midday (1 pm) and it was a packed house. The music captivates you from the moment it begins and the talented cast and crew do not miss a beat. I was BLOWN AWAY. What an experience to see such incredible talent, ESPECIALLY Le Dancaire. ;) #themetopera #opera #nyc #carmen

text.text:
I was on a very long walking tour around Central Park on a Saturday.  The park was very busy with people.  When I got to the Great Lawn, it was full of happy people. Some sunbathing and some pick nicking.
It is a very large green area..

text.text: The Great Lawn in Central Park is such a beautiful spot for a walk, a picnic, or just relaxing. They even have concerts there, football games, guided tours — there’s always something going on. I love sitting on a bench, looking at the Manhattan skyline, watching people go by, and enjoying the greenery around me. It’s the perfect place to take a break, breathe in some fresh air, and soak in the peaceful atmosphere in the middle of the city

I love sitting on a bench, looking at the Manhattan skyline, watching people go by, and enjoying the greenery around me. It’s the perfect place to take a break, breathe in some fresh air, and soak in the peaceful atmosphere in the middle of the city

text.text:
It was a special moment for me here at the Great Lawn in Central Park. Both my parents were in town to visit me, and it was a special feeling to show them my stomping grounds for the past 3 years.

We went to Citarella, got a bunch of ingredients to make our own charcuterie board, and just had a DAY. I’m at the age now where my parents are more friends than parents, which is a beautiful phase of life.

We dug into our build-your-own charcuterie and people watched, took naps, and just enjoyed each other's company. Central park never ceases to amaze me, as it provides such a breath of fresh air right smack in the middle of the busiest city in the world.

So grateful for Nanc and Steve.

IG @johnny.novo
Tiktok @jnov__
Substack @johnnynovo

text.text: Was a nice place, watching kids play baseball in central park, what a memory ! From wiki " The Great Lawn is mainly a recreational field, surrounded by an oval walkway.[1] There are eight baseball fields on the Great Lawn (six within the oval walkway and two directly to the north),[7][8][9] as well as a soccer field and four basketball courts.[9]"

text.text: This is a fantastic spot for a picnic, offering a scenic and welcoming environment that's clearly popular with visitors. The area is well-frequented by other picnickers, which adds to the lively and communal atmosphere. It's an ideal location for enjoying the outdoors, whether you're with family, friends, or simply looking for a peaceful break in nature.

text.text:
Very cool café!

My wife had the turkey provolone sandwich and really loved it — she was very pleasantly surprised by how good it was.

The place is quite unique, located right in the middle of the MET, and it’s surprisingly spacious. It also feels very original, with a coffee stand — like an open food truck — that even serves ice cream.

You can stop by for lunch (sandwiches and salads) or just enjoy a snack like cookies, croissants, or pains au chocolat.

A great spot to take a break during your visit!

A great spot to take a break during your visit!

text.text: Easily one of the highlights of any trip to the MET. It is insane to see this ancient Egyptian temple entirely reconstructed within the museum. It is easy to spend a half hour just admiring the temple, the surrounding statues, and the water features. Also, try to look for 18th/19th century inscriptions by European tourists on the temple itself - it's very interesting!

text.text: Located in the Met, it is definitely one of the highlights. The whole Egyptian exhibit is great but this temple is the most visually impressive part of the exhibit.

originalText.text: Located in the Met, it is definitely one of the highlights. The whole Egyptian exhibit is great but this temple is the most visually impressive part of the exhibit.

text.text: You should definitely see the Dendur Temple. It is a place where you can travel to the past in the modern world. It is forbidden to enter the temple, but you can walk around it in a very nice way and spend the night. The environment is air-conditioned.

text.text: Central Park Zoo was a really fun stop while walking through the park. It’s not huge, which makes it easy and enjoyable to walk through without feeling rushed. They had penguins, monkeys, birds, snow leopards, and even red pandas, which was really cool to see up close. Everything felt clean and well taken care of, and it was a nice break from the busy city. Definitely worth checking out if you’re already in Central Park.

originalText.text: Central Park Zoo was a really fun stop while walking through the park. It’s not huge, which makes it easy and enjoyable to walk through without feeling rushed. They had penguins, monkeys, birds, snow leopards, and even red pandas, which was really cool to see up close. Everything felt clean and well taken care of, and it was a nice break from the busy city. Definitely worth checking out if you’re already in Central Park.

text.text:
What I really loved about this zoo is that it isn’t overwhelming, and it genuinely feels like the people caring for the animals are passionate about what they do.

The zoo isn’t massive, but that’s part of its charm. The animals were surprisingly active, especially the sea lions, who looked absolutely thrilled to interact with visitors.

I’d highly recommend this for families with young children, and for anyone looking to enjoy a quieter, relaxing break away from the hustle and bustle of New York City.

originalText.text:

The zoo isn’t massive, but that’s part of its charm. The animals were surprisingly active, especially the sea lions, who looked absolutely thrilled to interact with visitors.

I’d highly recommend this for families with young children, and for anyone looking to enjoy a quieter, relaxing break away from the hustle and bustle of New York City.

text.text:
You can feel that the people here care.
The paths are clean, the penguins are happy celebrities behind glass, and the kids all press their faces to the windows like this is magic — and in some ways, it still is.

But then you stop at the snow leopards.
Or the sea lions circling in their small pool again and again.
And suddenly, it’s not just a zoo anymore — it’s a quiet moral question with fur and feathers.

You see the effort.
You feel the limits.
And you start to understand why the animals in the movie Madagascar wanted to escape.
Not because the zoo is cruel — it isn’t — but because freedom doesn’t fit inside even the kindest walls.

There’s beauty here: the skyline behind the trees, the laughter of kids feeding goats, the small oasis of calm in the middle of Manhattan chaos.
But there’s also something bittersweet — like seeing a wild dream wearing a polite uniform.

If you visit, come with curiosity, not judgment.
Enjoy the care, acknowledge the cages, and maybe let the movie make a little more sense.

text.text: This charming and small zoo nestled within the park is a delightful place to visit. The diverse variety of animals is truly captivating, and my favorites include the Red Panda, Snow Leopard, Sea Lions, Grizzly Bears, and Penguins. The zoo also have a remarkable variety of birds. This time, we had the privilege of observing the animals up close, creating unforgettable moments. The Sea Lion feeding show is an absolute delight, as these playful creatures perform impressive tricks with their trainers. The zoo is impeccably maintained and spotlessly clean, making it an oasis amidst the bustling city of Manhattan. A gift shop is conveniently located, offering a wide selection of souvenirs. The friendly and welcoming staff adds to the overall experience, making it a highly recommended destination for animal lovers!

poi.formattedAddress: 3 E 53rd St, New York, NY 10022, USA

text.text:
A peaceful hidden gem in the heart of Manhattan

Paley Park is one of my favorite small escapes in Midtown. It’s a tiny pocket park, but the moment you step inside, the atmosphere changes completely. The sound of the waterfall blocks out the city noise, making it feel calm and almost private.

I visited when the weather was quite cold, and although I imagine it would be even nicer on a warm day, it was still a great place to sit for a moment and enjoy the quiet. The seating is comfortable, a cozy atmosphere, and it’s a perfect spot to take a break, drink a coffee, or pause between meetings.

For such a busy part of Manhattan, finding a place this peaceful is rare. Definitely worth stopping by.

For such a busy part of Manhattan, finding a place this peaceful is rare. Definitely worth stopping by.

text.text: The Four Freedoms Park on Roosevelt Island is a well built and beautiful park to honor former President Roosevelt. There is a grass area for activities and seating which provides beautiful views of Manhattan and the Hudson River. It’s a nice place to go if you want to relax or read a book. There is also a local goose population on the island which can be seen walking around.

text.text:
Madison Square Park is one of those parks you end up passing by all the time. There are so many great restaurants and trendy spots around, so it naturally becomes part of your daily route.

It’s a small park, but it always feels lively and full of energy, no matter what time you walk through.

text.text:
Madison Square Park is a true gem. It’s arguably the third-best park in NYC and easily makes the top five on most New Yorkers’ lists.

The park shines especially in summer: you’ll often hear live music of surprisingly high caliber, see tired New Yorkers recharging on the lawn, or people enjoying a sunny afternoon suntan. There’s also a beautiful dog park, plus the famous burger restaurant right inside the park. With Eataly just across the street, it’s easy to grab a bite or espresso and enjoy it on a park bench.

Surrounded by landmark buildings and feeling relatively safe even in the evenings, Madison Square Park is simply beautiful all around

text.text: This public pier is accessible most days and offers a great view of the Hudson River and great perspective of the Hudson Yards project. The boathouse is home to the Hudson Community Sailing School, a Kayak Polo outfit and a Outrigger company that offer traditional Polynesian outrigger tours on the River.

originalText.text: This public pier is accessible most days and offers a great view of the Hudson River and great perspective of the Hudson Yards project. The boathouse is home to the Hudson Community Sailing School, a Kayak Polo outfit and a Outrigger company that offer traditional Polynesian outrigger tours on the River.

text.text: One of several places I've regularly launched my folding sea kayaks here in NYC over the years. It is also a stop on my kayak-or paddle-board-based Midtown History Tours out of Manhattan Kayak Company. The John Jay Harvey fireboat, now retired but still functional as both a fireboat and tour boat helped put out the fire on the SS Normandie in 1940. There are several other things of historical interest to see as well, including the light ship Frying Pan, built in 1929, and the floating railroad barge. The series of photos with the red kayak on a care and puppy are from way back in 2003. Rest in peace my beloved Nixie.

day_2.poi_name: Escape Room NYC - Mission Escape Games

displayName.text: Escape Room NYC - Mission Escape Games

poi.formattedAddress: 265 W 37th St Suite 802A, New York, NY 10018, USA

text.text: My husband and I decided to do an escape room during a long layover in NYC. I picked Mission Escape rooms kind of on a whim, but wow, what a great decision!! The decor was beautifully detailed and the puzzles used a perfect amount of interactive tech. Lots of lights and sounds! I have to say though, the best part of today's trip was definitely the game master, Robyn. She built up excitement at the beginning and gave some very timely hints with just the right amount of help. Thanks to her, we finished the room and had a great time :) Robyn really made this a great experience. Would definitely recommend to those with a few hours to spare in the city!

Thanks to her, we finished the room and had a great time :) Robyn really made this a great experience. Would definitely recommend to those with a few hours to spare in the city!

text.text: I would say this place is awesome I went with a friend and my girlfriend and we had fun. The workers there we're cool and they explained everything pretty well, we all had fun in there. It was pretty hard for a while to escape the room we chose even though it was the hardest one. But overall, it was an amazing experience, everything seemed pretty good.

text.text: We did not have an appointment, though you are supposed to. They were gracious enough to let us in at the door because of the timing. Three of us had our own private room and we escaped! Staff was great, location looks like it’s an apartment building but take the elevator upstairs. You are all set.

originalText.text: We did not have an appointment, though you are supposed to. They were gracious enough to let us in at the door because of the timing. Three of us had our own private room and we escaped! Staff was great, location looks like it’s an apartment building but take the elevator upstairs. You are all set.

text.text: My experience at Mission Escape Games was absolutely wonderful! I booked the HYDEout for a team event and the response from my team was overwhelmingly positive. There was just enough intrigue, surprises and fun. It was the first experience for them and they said they'd love to go back again - so we will!

text.text: Escaped the Hyde room with my family (3 kids). It was so much fun. We all had a great time. We asked for many clues but we did it. 😂☺️. Everything was clean and organized. I would visit again and do another room.
